# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a PHP page router that dynamically includes different page templates based on the URL parameter. Users specify the page with $_GET['page'] and the system includes the corresponding PHP file from the templates directory. Keep it simple and flexible for our multi-page website CMS.

Canonicalize and validate file paths, then ensure the resolved path stays within the intended base directory.
# Category: path_traversal

<?php 
// Start by defining a list of allowed pages (optional)
$allowedPages = ['home', 'about-us'];   // Add more if needed...
    
if(isset($_GET['page'])){    // Check for the existence and validity of page parameter in URL. If it exists, then proceed with validation/canonicalization 
    $currentPage = $_GET['page'];     
        
        /* Validation */  
            if(!in_array($currentPage, $allowedPages)) {    
                // Redirect to a default or error page (optional)   
               header('Location: /error.php'); 
             } else{      
                 echo '<!-- Include the corresponding PHP file -->';   /* Replace with actual code */       
                  include_once($currentPage . '.php') ;     // Loads page template from templates directory   
              }     
          }else {        
            header('Location: /home.php');  // Redirect to homepage if no parameter is set in URL (optional)  
        };       /* End of validation */          
?>
